title: Clockwork Angel
author: Cassandra Clare
blurb: Tessa Gray sails to Victorian London to find her brother — and is kidnapped instead by two shadow-warlocks who mean to weaponize her impossible gift. Rescued into the London Institute, she meets two Shadowhunters: the reckless, dying Will Herondale and the gentle, deadly Jem Carstairs. The trilogy that broke a generation's heart.

title: Clockwork Prince
author: Cassandra Clare
blurb: With the London Institute under investigation and Mortmain closing in, Tessa must decide between the two young men who love her — and every reader must decide which of them they can bear to break. The middle Infernal Devices book: dark, aching, and infamous.

title: Clockwork Princess
author: Cassandra Clare
blurb: War, illness, and a wedding neither Tessa nor Will can survive. The finale of The Infernal Devices is one of the most quoted, most-cried-over romantasy endings in the genre.

title: An Ember in the Ashes
author: Sabaa Tahir
blurb: Laia is a Scholar slave-girl who becomes a spy for the Resistance to save her brother; Elias is the finest soldier of the empire that owns her, and he wants out. Sabaa Tahir's Roman-inspired quartet is the modern romantasy standard for high-stakes forbidden love.

title: A Torch Against the Night
author: Sabaa Tahir
blurb: On the run from the empire that raised him, Elias races to keep Laia alive across a continent that wants them both dead. Tahir tightens every screw — new POVs, sharper stakes, a body count no one saw coming.

title: A Reaper at the Gates
author: Sabaa Tahir
blurb: With the empire tearing itself apart, Laia takes on the assassin who murdered her family, Helene faces the throne she was raised to serve, and Elias walks into the land of the dead. The third book: the one that hurts.

title: A Sky Beyond the Storm
author: Sabaa Tahir
blurb: Every ally, every enemy, every prophecy meets in one final battle for the empire and the world of the dead. Tahir's blazing conclusion to the Ember in the Ashes quartet.

title: Daughter of Smoke and Bone
author: Laini Taylor
blurb: Karou is a seventeen-year-old art student in Prague who runs errands for chimaera monsters and has no idea where she came from. When a scarred, terrible seraph named Akiva starts scorching handprints onto doorways, she is going to find out. Laini Taylor's gorgeous, moody, generation-defining opener.

title: Days of Blood and Starlight
author: Laini Taylor
blurb: Karou has become the resurrectionist of a broken chimaera rebellion; Akiva is a general in a seraph army she was raised to hate. The middle book of Laini Taylor's trilogy — bloodier, angrier, and full of the world-building the first hinted at.

title: Dreams of Gods and Monsters
author: Laini Taylor
blurb: Chimaera and seraph must ally, or nothing survives what is coming through the tears in the sky. Laini Taylor closes her trilogy with prose so lush people quote it a decade later.

title: Strange the Dreamer
author: Laini Taylor
blurb: Lazlo Strange, an orphan librarian who has dreamed of the lost city of Weep his entire life, joins the expedition sent to find it — and dreams a girl there who is not supposed to exist. Laini Taylor's most exquisite prose.

title: Muse of Nightmares
author: Laini Taylor
blurb: In the wake of Weep, Sarai must survive as one thing she was never supposed to be, and Lazlo must reckon with a name none of them anticipated. Taylor's Strange the Dreamer duology closes as beautifully as it began.

title: Cinder
author: Marissa Meyer
blurb: Cinder is a cyborg mechanic in plague-ridden New Beijing — and the emperor's son has just brought his broken android to her stall. Marissa Meyer's fairy-tale-meets-space-opera quartet begins with Cinderella and gets bigger every book.

title: Scarlet
author: Marissa Meyer
blurb: Scarlet Benoit hunts across France for her missing grandmother — with the help of a very hungry street fighter named Wolf. Meanwhile Cinder is breaking out of prison. The Lunar Chronicles' Red Riding Hood installment.

title: Cress
author: Marissa Meyer
blurb: A hacker locked alone in an orbiting satellite. A charming thief who is finally not lying. The Lunar Chronicles' Rapunzel arc: the crew grows, the stakes climb, the ships multiply.

title: Winter
author: Marissa Meyer
blurb: Princess Winter is losing her mind but never her kindness; Jacin is losing everything else to keep her safe. Meyer's blockbuster Snow White finale to The Lunar Chronicles — 800+ pages of payoff for every one of her four couples.

title: The Jasmine Throne
author: Tasha Suri
blurb: Princess Malini has been exiled to a haunted temple to atone for defying her tyrant brother; the maidservant Priya has secrets that could burn the entire empire down. Tasha Suri's Indian-inspired sapphic epic is the current gold standard for adult queer romantasy.

title: The Oleander Sword
author: Tasha Suri
blurb: Malini rides to war, Priya is elder now of a temple that answers to gods older than empire, and the yaksa have their own ideas about what these two are. Suri's middle book is a knife.

title: The Lotus Empire
author: Tasha Suri
blurb: One of them will be empress; one of them will be something the empire cannot survive. Suri closes the Burning Kingdoms with everything the trilogy has been threatening from page one.

title: Wicked Saints
author: Emily A. Duncan
blurb: Nadya can speak to gods; Serefin can raise the dead. Their kingdoms have been at war for a century, and the only way to end it may be to work together. A very dark, very Polish/Slavic-inspired romantasy that BookTok has never let go of.

title: Ruthless Gods
author: Emily A. Duncan
blurb: Nadya is losing her gods and her mind; Malachiasz is losing the man Nadya wanted him to be. Duncan's middle book pulls every fingernail out with love.

title: Blessed Monsters
author: Emily A. Duncan
blurb: The old gods, the fallen ones, and a country that never asked for any of them. The trilogy's blood-drenched finale.

title: Magic Bites
author: Ilona Andrews
blurb: Post-magic-apocalypse Atlanta runs on tech when the magic falters and monsters when it doesn't. Kate Daniels is a mercenary with a sword named Slayer and a very dangerous secret. And the local Beast Lord, Curran, has taken an unwelcome interest. The book that launched Ilona Andrews' 10-book urban fantasy romance.

title: Slave to Sensation
author: Nalini Singh
blurb: In a world where the Psy have chosen cold logic over emotion, Sascha Duncan is a defective Psy who feels — a secret that will get her executed. Then she is assigned to work with Lucas Hunter, alpha of a leopard shifter pack, who can smell her fear. Nalini Singh's foundational Psy-Changeling launch.

title: Angels' Blood
author: Nalini Singh
blurb: Elena Deveraux hunts vampires who break their contracts with angels; the archangel Raphael has just hired her to hunt one of his own. Nalini Singh's second foundational series — angels with claws, vampires as accessories, and an alpha romance that runs 15 books.

title: Dark Lover
author: J. R. Ward
blurb: The last purebred vampire warrior on earth needs a mate to carry on his line — but his fated is a half-human woman raised outside the shadow world he protects. J. R. Ward's Black Dagger Brotherhood: 20+ books of alpha vampires with muscle cars and modern R&B soundtracks. The book that shaped a genre.

title: A Hunger Like No Other
author: Kresley Cole
blurb: Emmaline Troy, a half-Valkyrie half-vampire who has never fed, walks straight into Lachlain MacRieve, the werewolf king who has spent a century in hell waiting for his mate. Kresley Cole's Immortals After Dark took paranormal romance and turned every dial to eleven.

title: Written in Red
author: Anne Bishop
blurb: Meg Corbyn is a cassandra sangue — a blood prophet — who has escaped the compound that farms her. She takes shelter in the Courtyard of the Others, run by beings who have never wanted humans this close. The book that launched Anne Bishop's beloved, slow-burning, found-family Others series.

title: The Goblin Emperor
author: Katherine Addison
blurb: The half-goblin son of an elvish emperor is called home from exile after an airship kills his entire family — and he is inexplicably the emperor now. A quiet, kind, deeply-satisfying cult-favorite standalone about a boy learning to be good at a job no one expected him to inherit.

title: Assassin's Apprentice
author: Robin Hobb
blurb: FitzChivalry Farseer is the bastard son of a prince — raised in the stables, trained in secret to kill, and bound by a magic he was never supposed to have. Robin Hobb's Farseer trilogy is the reason a generation of romantasy readers know what real slow burn feels like.

title: Jade City
author: Fonda Lee
blurb: On the island of Kekon, magical jade is worn only by trained warriors — and the two families that control it are about to go to war. Fonda Lee's Green Bone Saga: The Godfather crossed with wuxia, with a romance thread that makes book two devastating.

title: Black Sun
author: Rebecca Roanhorse
blurb: A crow-eyed young man is being sailed across the world to arrive on the winter solstice and finish an old prophecy in Tova. The sea captain who carries him has secrets of her own. Rebecca Roanhorse's pre-Columbian-inspired Between Earth and Sky trilogy is one of the most vivid worlds in modern epic fantasy.

title: The Beautiful
author: Renée Ahdieh
blurb: Celine Rousseau flees Paris to a convent in 1872 New Orleans — a city ruled by two immortal courts, one of which has just started leaving bodies at her door. Sébastien Saint Germain is the only person she should not want. Ahdieh's Gothic New Orleans vampire romance.

title: Flame in the Mist
author: Renée Ahdieh
blurb: On her way to a marriage she never wanted, Mariko's convoy is ambushed and everyone dies except her. Disguising herself as a boy, she infiltrates the outlaw Black Clan that tried to kill her. Renée Ahdieh's Japanese-inspired Mulan retelling.

title: Mexican Gothic
author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
blurb: In 1950s Mexico, glamorous Noemí Taboada is dispatched to a decaying mining-country mansion to rescue her cousin from the strange English family she married into. What lives in the walls of High Place has been waiting a very long time. Bestselling gothic horror-romance.

title: The Witch's Heart
author: Genevieve Gornichec
blurb: Angrboda is a witch who once refused Odin. In exile she takes a wandering trickster to bed and bears him three children — Fenrir, Hel, and Jörmungandr, the three creatures fated to end the gods. Genevieve Gornichec's aching, feminist Norse retelling.

title: The Stardust Thief
author: Chelsea Abdullah
blurb: Loulie al-Nazari is a smuggler of magical jinn relics who is very good at not getting caught; the sultan's son who arrests her demands one impossible artifact in exchange for her life. Middle-Eastern-inspired epic romantasy modeled on the Arabian Nights.

title: House of Salt and Sorrows
author: Erin A. Craig
blurb: The Thaumas sisters live in a decaying cliffside manor by the sea; four of the twelve of them are already dead. When Annaleigh's dreams start filling with warnings, she has to find out what's really killing her family. Craig's dark, sea-soaked Twelve Dancing Princesses retelling.

title: Air Awakens
author: Elise Kova
blurb: Vhalla Yarl is a library apprentice who has never touched anything more dangerous than a book — until she saves the crown prince's life and is dragged into a magical inheritance she never wanted. Elise Kova's beloved five-book series that launched her career.

title: The Bone Shard Daughter
author: Andrea Stewart
blurb: On an empire of islands that migrate across the sea, the emperor's daughter is quietly relearning the forbidden bone-shard magic her father uses to control the world — while every island prepares to rebel. Andrea Stewart's inventive sapphic epic.
